title: 9 Reasons For Rise In DoorDash Market Share
text: DoorDash commands an impressive 67-68% market share in the U.S. food delivery sector as of 2025, more than double its nearest competitor. By the end of 2024, DoorDash led the national food delivery market with a 60.7% share, ahead of Uber Eats at 26.1% and Grubhub at 6.3%. DoorDash commands 67% US market share by targeting underserved suburban markets competitors ignored, achieving lower acquisition costs and higher retention. DoorDash maintains a 58% market share in suburban markets, exceeding its overall national average. Advanced AI and machine learning optimize deliveries, process 7M daily orders, and create competitive moats through data advantages competitors cannot replicate. In Q3 2025, the company achieved revenue of $3.446 billion, up 27% year-over-year, with Adjusted EBITDA reaching $754 million, a 41% increase from the prior year period. Uber Eats holds approximately 23-24% market share with a global leader position, while Grubhub maintains 6-8% market share focused on the U.S. only. However, in the New York City area, DoorDash at 38.4% and Uber Eats at 38.2% were nearly tied by the end of 2024. The company projects marketplace gross order value growth between 17-20% in 2025, indicating continued expansion despite market maturity. DoorDash is diversifying through initiatives like DoorDash Drive and DashMart, which have allowed expansion into grocery, alcohol, and retail item deliveries.
